title: French Kitchen
editorialSummary: Classic French cooking techniques sorted by method: dry-heat (sauté, roast, flambé, grill), moist-heat (braise, poach, steam, blanch), fat-based (confit, deep-fry, baste, render), and sauce-building (deglaze, reduce, mount, emulsify). Each group is a single canonical method family.

meta.gptRationale: Cooking methods are learnable, but the French-kitchen framing and technical distinctions between heat/fat/sauce processes raise the precision needed.
